ja-gram-n3-649-fill-0: にする chooses from available options (setting/selecting). Unlike ことになる (passive—decided by external factors), にする is active—you decide. Unlike ようになる (gradual change), it's decisive.
ja-gram-n3-649-fill-1: にする means to decide on/choose—fits the conditional sense of 'if you do it this way.' Differs from ことにする (intend to do), which adds volitional future action; here it's a choice made.
ja-gram-n3-1732-fill-0: ことになる expresses passive decision—it has been arranged/decided by circumstances, not by you. Contrasts with にする (active choice) and ことにする (personal intention).
ja-gram-n3-1732-fill-1: ことになる shows external arrangement—the outcome was decided by formal decision/natural consequence. Unlike ことにする (personal intent), this emphasizes fate/official arrangement.
ja-gram-n3-687-fill-1: になる indicates state change—becoming sick from inattention. Fits the result structure 'cause → state change.' Different from ことになる (arranged outcome) and ことにする (personal decision).
ja-gram-n3-687-fill-2: になる expresses inevitable state—will become terrible. It's a future state resulting from the situation. Unlike ことになる (passive arrangement), it's pure transformation.
ja-gram-n3-687-fill-3: になる shows aspiration toward a state—wanting to become teacher. Unlike ことにする (decide to do action), it targets becoming a role/state.
ja-gram-n3-1304-fill-0: さえ emphasizes extreme case—'even a strawberry.' Shows something so unexpected/extreme that even this won't work. Unlike しか (requires negative), さえ works with positive negation here.
ja-gram-n3-1304-fill-1: さえ marks an extreme scenario—'even eat a mouse.' It highlights how desperate/extreme the situation is. Different from くらい (degree) and しか (only with negation).
ja-gram-n3-1304-fill-2: さえ emphasizes surprise—'don't even know existence.' The particle stresses how extreme the ignorance is. Unlike ばかり (just did), さえ marks a surprising low point.
ja-gram-n3-1304-fill-3: さえ shows even an extreme task fails—'can't even find source code.' It marks frustration at the basic failure. Unlike しか (negation-exclusive), さえ allows conditional negation.
ja-gram-n3-1304-fill-4: さえ expresses concession—'even using a ruler.' Despite using the tool, the task still fails. Unlike しか, it doesn't require verb negation.
ja-gram-n3-1199-fill-0: しか requires negative verb—'can only write romaji.' The particle itself signals exclusivity; the verb must be negative. Unlike さえ (works with many verb forms), しか is strictly negative-only.
ja-gram-n3-1199-fill-1: しか means 'only/except'—'only Tanaka came' (via negation). With negative verb, it limits to one option. Different from さえ (no negation required) and ばかり (just did).
ja-gram-n3-1199-fill-2: しか marks singular amount—'only 100 yen.' Negative verb is required. Unlike くらい (approximate degree), しか is strict limitation.
ja-gram-n3-1199-fill-3: しか restricts to one category—'nothing but red things.' With negative verb, it's exclusive. Unlike ばかり (recent action), しか modifies nouns for exclusivity.
ja-gram-n3-1199-fill-4: しか requires negation to mean 'only'—'can only go by car.' The negative verb is mandatory. Unlike さえ, which doesn't need negation, しか is negative-bound.
ja-gram-n3-253-fill-0: こそ intensifies—'It IS love that strengthens people.' Particle emphasizes THIS specific thing. Unlike しか (requires negative) and さえ (marks extremes), こそ highlights importance.
ja-gram-n3-253-fill-1: こそ intensifies focus—'THIS time I'll do it.' Stresses determination about this specific moment. Different from わ (feminine emphasis) and ばかり (just did).
ja-gram-n3-253-fill-2: こそ emphasizes core truth—'Dreams ARE the source of hope.' Marks what's fundamentally important. Unlike って (quotation), こそ is pure emphasis.
ja-gram-n3-253-fill-3: こそ stresses responsibility—'It is I who must apologize.' Points to the subject deserving focus. Unlike しか (requires negation), こそ works affirmatively.
ja-gram-n3-253-fill-4: こそ highlights priority—'Family IS the most important.' The particle marks what truly matters. Unlike ばかり (only did), こそ emphasizes essential truth.
ja-gram-n3-1595-fill-0: くらい/ぐらい shows degree—'so warm (we) don't need heat.' Indicates extent/degree of situation. Unlike ばかり (just did), くらい measures degree magnitude.
ja-gram-n3-1595-fill-1: くらい marks extreme degree—'so tired (we) couldn't walk.' Shows intensity. Unlike さえ (extreme case), くらい measures the degree itself.
ja-gram-n3-1595-fill-2: くらい compares degree—'as forgetful as Yamada.' Marks equivalence in degree. Unlike より (comparison), くらい indicates matching degree level.
ja-gram-n3-1595-fill-3: くらい shows minimal ability—'I can cook rice, at least.' Marks the lowest degree of competence. Different from さえ (extreme case) and ばかり (just started).
ja-gram-n3-1595-fill-4: くらい indicates capability level—'He's the one capable (of this).' Shows sufficient degree/competence. Unlike しか (requires negation), くらい works affirmatively.
ja-gram-n3-1502-fill-0: ばかり marks 'just did'—'having just gone to Japan.' Refers to recent completion. Unlike くらい (degree), ばかり is time-recent.
ja-gram-n3-1502-fill-1: ばかり indicates recent action—'just ate, so wait.' The particle marks immediate past. Different from くらい (degree) and こそ (emphasis).
ja-gram-n3-1502-fill-2: ばかり means 'just began'—'had just started working.' Marks recent beginning. Unlike ばかり meaning 'only/nothing but,' this is temporal.
ja-gram-n3-752-fill-0: よ asserts new information—'The bus is coming!' Marks speaker assertion/emphasis. Unlike わ (feminine), よ is gender-neutral assertion.
ja-gram-n3-752-fill-1: よ emphasizes a serious statement—'That's serious (you know)!' Adds force/assertion. Different from わ (softer, female) and ってば (insistent).
ja-gram-n3-752-fill-2: よ asserts confession—'I like you, you know!' Speaker emphasizes declaration. Unlike なあ (reflection) and ってば (pleading), よ is straightforward assertion.
ja-gram-n3-752-fill-3: よ commands with force—'Shape up!' Adds strong assertion. Unlike わ (mild) and ってば (emotional), よ is direct.
ja-gram-n3-752-fill-4: よ affirms agreement—'Sure!' Asserts acceptance. Unlike ってば (insistent), よ is calm assertion.
ja-gram-n3-652-fill-0: なあ expresses emotional longing—'If only we could fish tomorrow!' Reflects desire/sentiment. Unlike よ (factual assertion), なあ is emotional.
ja-gram-n3-652-fill-1: なあ shows emotional wish—'I'd love a cold beer.' The particle expresses yearning. Different from よ (factual) and わ (feminine).
ja-gram-n3-652-fill-2: なあ marks emotional state—'I'm so hungry.' Expresses feeling/emotion. Unlike よ (assertion) and ってば (insistence), なあ is reflective.
ja-gram-n3-879-fill-0: って marks indirect quotation—'They say love is blind.' Reports what others said. Unlike は (particle), って functions as quotation marker.
ja-gram-n3-879-fill-1: って introduces reported speech—'I said, "turn down TV."' Marks what was actually said. Different from は (subject particle) and よ (assertion).
ja-gram-n3-879-fill-2: って presents hearsay—'Is this the key he was looking for?' Implies reported or understood information. Unlike よ (assertion), って is indirect.
ja-gram-n3-879-fill-3: って marks indirect report—'said he didn't know.' Quotes without direct speaker attribution. Different from は (particle) and よ (emphatic).
ja-gram-n3-879-fill-4: って in conditional—'no matter how much asked, can't do it.' Shows 'no matter what'—universal negation. Shifts from quotation to concessive.
ja-gram-n3-776-fill-0: わ adds feminine emphasis—'That's just fine.' Softer than よ, distinctly female. Unlike よ (neutral assertion), わ is gendered.
ja-gram-n3-776-fill-1: わ brings feminine tone—'This book is interesting (F).' The particle softens assertion. Different from よ (neutral) and ってば (insistent).
ja-gram-n3-776-fill-2: わ marks feminine speech—'I don't have money in my wallet.' The particle is characteristically female. Unlike よ (gender-neutral), わ is explicitly feminine.
ja-gram-n3-1516-fill-0: ってば expresses emotional insistence—'I said I would go!' Repeats with emotional force. Unlike って (simple quotation), ってば adds insistence.
ja-gram-n3-1516-fill-1: ってば marks emphatic command—'I told you go home!' Repeats for emotional emphasis. Different from よ (assertion) and わ (soft).
ja-gram-n3-1516-fill-2: ってば expresses exasperation—'What's that!' Marks frustration/emotion. Unlike って (neutral quotation), ってば adds emotional charge.
ja-gram-n3-1516-fill-3: ってば insists emotionally—'I'm telling you, Marle's fine!' Repeats for emotional force. Different from よ (factual) and わ (female).
ja-gram-n3-1686-fill-0: ~ぶり marks time span elapsed—'after a long time returned home.' Shows duration since last occurrence. Unlike 直す (redo), ぶり is temporal.
ja-gram-n3-1686-fill-1: ~ぶり indicates years elapsed—'after 3 years returned.' Marks time passage since last event. Different from 直す (redo) and に従って (follow).
ja-gram-n3-868-fill-0: ところで shifts topic—'It's raining. By the way, want to eat?' Moves conversation sideways. Unlike それに (adds info), ところで resets discussion.
ja-gram-n3-868-fill-1: ところで in alternate sense—'No matter how much I persuade, he won't listen.' Shows concessive meaning. Different from その上 (moreover) and そうすると (consequence).
ja-gram-n3-868-fill-2: ところで introduces new topic—'By the way, what's your job?' Signals conversation shift. Unlike それに (connects), ところで diverges.
ja-gram-n3-868-fill-3: ところで changes subject—'By the way, did you do homework?' Marks topic transition. Different from その上 (addition) and さもないと (or else).
ja-gram-n3-868-fill-4: ところで opens new point—'By the way, I have something to tell you.' Shifts focus. Unlike そうすると (logical consequence), ところで is independent.
ja-gram-n3-1711-fill-3: その上 shows addition—'To make matters worse, strong wind blew.' Piles negative info. Unlike ところで (topic shift), その上 adds sequentially.
ja-gram-n3-1711-fill-4: その上 means moreover—'He speaks English. Additionally, French.' Adds supporting information. Different from それに (adds info but weaker) and ところで (topic shift).
ja-gram-n3-1706-fill-0: さもないと warns consequence—'Apologize. Otherwise, I'll be angry.' Shows threat/result of inaction. Unlike その上 (addition), さもないと is conditional threat.
ja-gram-n3-1706-fill-1: さもないと shows inevitable consequence—'Get this bus, or I'll be late.' Otherwise triggers outcome. Different from それに (adds) and ところで (shifts).
ja-gram-n3-1706-fill-2: さもないと warns of result—'I must hurry, or miss the bus.' Otherwise-clause consequence. Unlike その上 (addition), さもないと is conditional.
ja-gram-n3-1706-fill-3: さもないと threatens outcome—'Button up, or you'll catch cold.' Otherwise-path consequence. Different from それに (adds info) and ましてや (let alone).
ja-gram-n3-1706-fill-4: さもないと warns consequence—'Study English, or fail test.' Otherwise-result. Unlike その上 (addition), さもないと is conditional threat.
ja-gram-n3-1520-fill-0: ましてや escalates—'Can't admit to self, much less to others.' Intensifies by adding harder case. Unlike さもないと (or else), ましてや escalates difficulty.
ja-gram-n3-1520-fill-1: ましてや intensifies negation—'Can't play guitar, much less violin.' Adds harder impossibility. Different from その上 (addition) and ところで (shift).
ja-gram-n3-1520-fill-2: ましてや escalates—'Won't lend watch to anyone, much less part with it.' Intensifies from harder-to-harder. Unlike ところで (shift), ましてや piles on.
ja-gram-n3-1520-fill-3: ましてや escalates negation—'Can't afford used car, much less new.' Stacks impossible scenarios. Different from さもないと (or else) and ひいては (turns to).
ja-gram-n3-1520-fill-4: ましてや intensifies ability—'Speaks French, and furthermore English.' Escalates positively from good to better. Unlike それに (just adds), ましてや stresses escalation.
ja-gram-n3-1407-fill-0: そうすると shows logical consequence—'Meeting girls at 9? That means no beer time!' So-then draws conclusion. Unlike その上 (addition), そうすると is logical.
ja-gram-n3-1407-fill-1: そうすると marks resulting state—'That makes you even more attractive.' If-then consequence. Different from ところで (shift) and ひいては (in turn).
ja-gram-n3-1681-fill-0: ひいては shows chain result—'Good for mother's body, which in turn good for baby.' Links cause→effect→effect. Unlike ましてや (escalates), ひいては chains.
ja-gram-n3-1681-fill-1: ひいては shows downstream effect—'Will this in turn lead to internationalization?' Traces consequence chain. Different from そうすると (direct logic) and その上 (addition).
ja-gram-n3-1675-fill-0: と同様 compares similarity—'Like many Americans, he's friendly.' Shows same-way likeness. Unlike とは限らない (not always), と同様 asserts similarity.
ja-gram-n3-516-fill-0: とは限らない shows exception—'Expensive things aren't always better.' Negates automatic assumption. Unlike と同様 (similarity), とは限らない allows variation.
ja-gram-n3-516-fill-1: とは限らない allows flexibility—'You can't always have your way.' Exception to rule. Different from 並みに (same degree) and と同様 (similarity).
ja-gram-n3-516-fill-2: とは限らない opens possibility—'It's not always easy to make good jokes.' Denies automatic consequence. Unlike ましてや (escalates), とは限らない allows exceptions.
ja-gram-n3-516-fill-3: とは限らない negates assumption—'Delicious-looking food doesn't necessarily taste good.' Allows variation from expectation. Different from と同様 (similarity) and 並みに (same degree).
ja-gram-n3-516-fill-4: とは限らない denies causation—'Money doesn't always bring happiness.' Allows for variation. Unlike と同様 (sameness), とは限らない permits exception.
ja-gram-n3-1682-fill-0: 並みに shows degree equality—'Wikipedia is as accurate as Britannica.' Matches degree level. Unlike とは限らない (exception), 並みに asserts equivalence.
ja-gram-n3-780-fill-0: なら presents conditional—'If you're taking from outside, that's okay.' Assumes situation given. Unlike ば (hypothetical), なら is situational-if.
ja-gram-n3-780-fill-1: なら shows situational if—'If you drive, don't drink.' Assumes situation exists. Different from ば (hypothetical focus) and と (automatic consequence).
ja-gram-n3-780-fill-2: なら expresses preference—'Rather die than hide beliefs.' Shows conditional choice. Unlike としても (even if), なら is situational-if.
ja-gram-n3-780-fill-3: なら presents condition—'If you can't attend (for unavoidable reasons), refund due.' Situational if. Different from ば (hypothetical) and と (automatic).
ja-gram-n3-780-fill-4: なら shows conditional policy—'If you can't attend (for personal reasons), no refund.' Assumes situation. Unlike と (automatic), なら is conditional-if.
ja-gram-n3-1368-fill-0: ~と marks automatic result—'When you see Romeo & Juliet, you definitely cry.' Shows natural/automatic consequence. Unlike なら (situational), と is automatic-if.
